item.title: #ad Most people don’t realize there’s an entire industry built around collecting...
item.description:
@addielamarr.sh

#ad
Most people don’t realize there’s an entire industry built around collecting and selling your personal data.

Data brokers compile things like your name, address history, phone number, and even relatives, then sell that data to people-search sites. Once your profile exists, it gets copied, repackaged, and resold across the internet.

That creates two problems
• Social engineering because bad actors can sound more credible
• Exposure because strangers can easily look you up online

Even if you remove yourself from one site, your info can come back when brokers republish new data.

That’s why I’ve been a paying DeleteMe customer for the past 1.5+ years, and after using it extensively myself I decided to partner with them.

DeleteMe removes your personal information from hundreds of data broker sites and keeps monitoring so it doesn’t just reappear later.

I’m sending the link to anyone who:
✅ comments “HUSH”
✅ follows @addielamarr.sh (privacy settings can block the DM if you don’t)

Or get 20% off DeleteMe by going to joindeleteme.com/ADDIE and using code ADDIE to protect your privacy! @joindeleteme
item.content:encoded:
@addielamarr.sh

#ad
Most people don’t realize there’s an entire industry built around collecting and selling your personal data.

Data brokers compile things like your name, address history, phone number, and even relatives, then sell that data to people-search sites. Once your profile exists, it gets copied, repackaged, and resold across the internet.

That creates two problems
• Social engineering because bad actors can sound more credible
• Exposure because strangers can easily look you up online

Even if you remove yourself from one site, your info can come back when brokers republish new data.

That’s why I’ve been a paying DeleteMe customer for the past 1.5+ years, and after using it extensively myself I decided to partner with them.

DeleteMe removes your personal information from hundreds of data broker sites and keeps monitoring so it doesn’t just reappear later.

I’m sending the link to anyone who:
✅ comments “HUSH”
✅ follows @addielamarr.sh (privacy settings can block the DM if you don’t)

Or get 20% off DeleteMe by going to joindeleteme.com/ADDIE and using code ADDIE to protect your privacy! @joindeleteme
item.author: addielamarr.sh

item.title: Your devices are never truly off. Hidden processors like Intel’s ME and Apple’s ...
item.description:
@addielamarr.sh

Your devices are never truly off. Hidden processors like Intel’s ME and Apple’s Secure Enclave stay on, tracking data even when powered down. These closed-source chips can’t be disabled, making them ripe for exploitation—like the CIA’s Weeping Angel hack on Samsung TVs. The only real solution? Open-source hardware and de-Googled phones. But most people don’t even know they exist.

Xoxo,
Addie💕

#cybersecurity #InfoSec
#Tech #TechTips #techcareer #technology #internet #coding #womenintech #coding #education #IT #hacker #CybersecurityJob

Did you know about these co-processors before this video?
item.content:encoded:
@addielamarr.sh

Your devices are never truly off. Hidden processors like Intel’s ME and Apple’s Secure Enclave stay on, tracking data even when powered down. These closed-source chips can’t be disabled, making them ripe for exploitation—like the CIA’s Weeping Angel hack on Samsung TVs. The only real solution? Open-source hardware and de-Googled phones. But most people don’t even know they exist.

Xoxo,
Addie💕

#cybersecurity #InfoSec
#Tech #TechTips #techcareer #technology #internet #coding #womenintech #coding #education #IT #hacker #CybersecurityJob

Did you know about these co-processors before this video?
item.author: addielamarr.sh

item.title: We need to have a serious conversation right now—especially for marginalized com...
item.description:
@addielamarr.sh

We need to have a serious conversation right now—especially for marginalized communities. With new laws and policies targeting personal freedoms, your text messages could get you into real trouble.

Why?

Because text messages aren’t sent directly from phone to phone. Instead, they pass through a Short Message Service Center (SMSC), where they’re stored, scanned, and left vulnerable to surveillance.

If you’re seeking healthcare, have a friend who is undocumented, are part of the queer community, or involved in any kind of resistance, your messages could be used against you.

The solution? Stop using your phone’s native texting app immediately. Switch to apps like Signal or Threema (for all messages, not just sensitive ones), which offer end-to-end encryption to keep your conversations private. Your safety and privacy are worth the change.

Top: @uniquevintage

Xoxo,
Addie💕

#cybersecurity #InfoSec
#Tech #TechTips #techcareer #technology #internet #coding #womenintech #coding #education #IT #hacker #CybersecurityJob

Do you use your phone’s native messaging app?
item.content:encoded:
@addielamarr.sh

We need to have a serious conversation right now—especially for marginalized communities. With new laws and policies targeting personal freedoms, your text messages could get you into real trouble.

Why?

Because text messages aren’t sent directly from phone to phone. Instead, they pass through a Short Message Service Center (SMSC), where they’re stored, scanned, and left vulnerable to surveillance.

If you’re seeking healthcare, have a friend who is undocumented, are part of the queer community, or involved in any kind of resistance, your messages could be used against you.

The solution? Stop using your phone’s native texting app immediately. Switch to apps like Signal or Threema (for all messages, not just sensitive ones), which offer end-to-end encryption to keep your conversations private. Your safety and privacy are worth the change.

Top: @uniquevintage

Xoxo,
Addie💕
